title: 10 best herbal essential oils for bleeding gums
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for bleeding gums are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate bleeding gums by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healing. The best herbal essential oils for bleeding gums include Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Syzygium aromaticum, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Mentha x piperita, Salvia officinalis, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Cymbopogon citratus, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, bleeding gums can lead to tooth loss, pain, and embarrassment, negatively impacting daily life, social interactions, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's main compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to reduce bacteria growth in the mouth, which can cause gum inflammation and bleeding. Additionally, tea tree oil's ability to combat fungal infections can help to alleviate symptoms of gum disease. When used topically with a carrier oil or in a mouthwash, tea tree essential oils can provide effective relief from bleeding gums by reducing swelling and promoting healthy gum tissue regeneration.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help combat the underlying infections causing bleeding gums.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum inflammation and bleeding.
Wound-healing properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil contains compounds that may promote wound healing, helping to accelerate the recovery process of damaged gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Tea tree leaves contain a high concentration of compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the tea tree plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess antiseptic and analgesic properties, making them effective against bleeding gums and toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia in the early morning for optimal potency and freshness.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and contaminants then dry with paper towels.
Steam distill the dried leaves at a temperature range of 100-110°c for approximately 2-3 hours using a high-quality still.
Separate the essential oil from the resulting condensate by decantation or filtration techniques within 30 minutes.
Bottle and store the obtained essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The oil's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial effects combat bacterial infections that can contribute to bleeding gums. By applying chamomile essential oils topically or taking it orally, you can effectively alleviate symptoms of bleeding gums, promoting a healthier mouth and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile essential oil reduces inflammation in the gum tissue, which helps to alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with bleeding gums.
Antibacterial properties: Chamomile essential oil's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause bleeding gums by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.

Soothing and calming properties: The calming nature of chamomile essential oil helps to reduce anxiety, stress, and discomfort associated with bleeding gums, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in bleeding gums.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly by rinsing in cool water.
Steep the flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 20 minutes to release their bioactive compounds and flavor.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a bowl, squeezing gently to extract as much oil as possible.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil to the extracted liquid and mix well for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 hours before filtering it again through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its potent antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat gum infections. Its antiseptic qualities also promote a healthy environment in the mouth, preventing the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease and bleeding. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm the gum tissue, reducing discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: English Lavender oil's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and bleeding, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the gums, which helps to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums.
Antioxidant property: English Lavender oil's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to bleeding, promoting healthy gum tissue growth and repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bleeding in gums.
Seeds: The seeds of English lavender are rich in fatty acids and terpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom at dawn when dew is still present for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm and dry environment away from direct sunlight using paper bags or cloth sacks.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of pure water at 212 degrees fahrenheit for one hour.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled plant material using a separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottles.
Mix 10 drops of the freshly extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil with 1 teaspoon of coconut oil or olive oil and apply to bleeding gums twice daily as needed for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When used in a mouthwash or added to toothpaste, peppermint oil helps reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation. Its menthol content also stimulates blood flow and helps to dislodge trapped bacteria, promoting healthy gum tissue regeneration. Additionally, peppermint's antiseptic properties combat infection-causing germs, accelerating the healing process and preventing further bleeding.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacterial infections that cause gum inflammation and bleeding, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint oil can reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with bleeding gums, making it easier to manage discomfort and promote healing.
Astringent properties: Peppermint essential oil's astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: The buds of peppermint contain menthol, a natural antibacterial agent that helps combat bacterial infections that can cause bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local nursery measuring approximately 100 grams.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 7 hours.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves using a distillation apparatus containing 500 milliliters of water and 10 milligrams of calcium hydroxide for 30 minutes.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled mixture using a separating funnel and measuring approximately 20 milliliters.
Bottle and store the final mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass container with a tight-fitting lid for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected area. The oil's antibacterial agents also combat the bacteria that contribute to gum disease, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's natural antiseptic properties help to soothe and protect the gums, accelerating healing and reducing bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of the oil reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage bleeding and discomfort.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile compounds and flavonoids, making it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 50g of clean leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and allow it to steep for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to decant for 2 days before transferring to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's ability to combat bacteria and fungi that cause gum inflammation and infection, while also reducing swelling and pain, makes it an effective natural remedy for treating bleeding gums. Additionally, geranium oil's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels, which can reduce bleeding and promote healthy gum tissue growth.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed or irritated gums, promoting a healthy healing environment.

Astringent: As an astringent, geranium essential oil has the ability to constrict blood vessels and help stop bleeding by reducing capillary fragility, which can be beneficial for treating bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, geranium roots have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation and bleeding due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, geranium barks are believed to help reduce gum swelling and alleviate pain associated with bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves from well-watered plants during early morning hours when dew still present.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a clean cloth or paper towels within 30 minutes of harvesting.
Steam distill the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves in a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 4-6 hours with 1 cup water to 10 cups leaves ratio.
Collect the essential oil that condenses in the distillation receiver and filter it through a coffee filter or cotton cloth within 2 hours of distillation.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a trusted source, ensure freshness and quality.
Dry the collected plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low temperature.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried thymus vulgaris using 500 milliliters of water for one hour and fifteen minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate from the water.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keep refrigerated at 3-4°c for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a copper still at 100°c for 3 hours.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Decant the clear essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 2 weeks before using it topically.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, penetrate deep into the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and combating bacterial infections that can cause bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning for optimal oil quality and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours or until crumbly.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed leaves in a glass still using 5% water content and collect the oil over 2 hours.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the gums, promoting healthy oral tissue. Additionally, ginger's antibacterial properties help combat bad breath and gum infections that can contribute to bleeding gums. By using herbal ginger essential oils, individuals can alleviate discomfort, promote healing, and maintain a healthy smile.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, thereby minimizing bleeding.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial activity of herbal ginger essential oil can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to gum inflammation and bleeding, promoting a healthy oral environment.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately two pounds.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm dry place for at least seven days ensuring complete dehydration.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the ground ginger powder with 30ml of water for 45 minutes to extract essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove impurities and yield pure zingiber officinale essential oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bleeding gums with herbal essential oils is by mixing a few drops of peppermint oil and tea tree oil with coconut oil. Gently massage the blend onto your gums using a soft-bristled toothbrush, twice daily. This helps reduce inflammation and bacteria growth, promoting healing and relief from bleeding.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bleeding gums that are treated with herbal essential oils are gum inflammation, toothaches, and mouth sores. Certain essential oils like tea tree oil, eucalyptus oil, and peppermint oil have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat these issues. They can be used in a mouthwash or applied topically to the affected area to reduce pain and promote healing. Additionally, these oils can also soothe sore throats and freshen breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections and bleeding. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help to reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the gums, facilitating healing and reducing bleeding. remedy_constituents: Cineole: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in bleeding gums.
Eugenia-2,4-diene: This sesquiterpene has potent antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections in the gums that may be causing the bleeding.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaved essential oil is obtained through steam distillation and used for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and treat bleeding gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves in large quantities preferably in early morning to obtain highest essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to prevent mold.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a distillation apparatus for 3 to 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil using a filter paper to remove any impurities and obtain a clear final product.

The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gums and promote healing. By reducing bacterial growth and inflammation, the oil helps to combat the underlying causes of bleeding gums, such as gingivitis and periodontal disease. remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they are rich in antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and heal bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as caryophyllene and beta-caryophyllene, have been shown to combat the bacteria responsible for gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment. remedy_parts: Buds: Used due to their high content of eugenol, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that helps soothe and heal bleeding gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of syzygium aromaticum flowers on a dry sunny morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the flowers in a 1:3 ratio with water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled water and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting caps for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, the oil promotes healthy gum tissue and reduces the risk of bleeding. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums, thereby preventing bleeding.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems are rich in gingerols and shogaols, compounds with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bleeding gums.

Stems: The stems of the plant contain essential oils that help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria in the gums, addressing the issue of bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 mature rhizomes of zingiber officinale, ensuring they are free from any signs of decay.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly under running water to remove any impurities.
Steam distillation is used to extract the essential oil from the rhizomes at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with bleeding gums because it contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that combat the underlying causes of gum inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 2-3 hours at 100-110°c to release their essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and filter it to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent oxidation.
Label the bottle with the date of distillation the batch number and the name of the plant used.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Linalool: This terpene constituent exhibits antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation, thereby reducing bleeding.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the gums from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healing of bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil from Mentha x piperita leaves is commonly used to make oils for bleeding gums due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: The essential oil from Mentha x piperita stems is used for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and heal bleeding gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20% of mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from mature plants during peak oil production hours.
Steam distill 10 kg of harvested mentha x piperita in a 30-liter distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for 24 hours.
Analyze the final product using gc-ms to ensure purity and quality of the mentha x piperita essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thujone and camphor, work together to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum disease. Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing bleeding.

Bornyl acetate: A terpene with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that helps prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing and reducing bleeding.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from the salvia officinalis plant at dawn to ensure optimal oil content and freshness.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris for better oil extraction.
Steam distill the leaves in a copper still using 3:1 water to herb ratio for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a clean glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove impurities and ensure clarity.

The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease, while also reducing inflammation and swelling in the gums. This helps to prevent bleeding and promotes healthy gum tissue regeneration. remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, reducing bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums that can lead to bleeding.

Barks: The barks are another widely used part, as they contain cinnamaldehyde, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used to produce essential oils, as they contain a high concentration of eugenol and other compounds that help to soothe and protect the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves and stems by cutting them with pruning shears.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a condenser and separator.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed tightly to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It has the ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections that can cause bleeding gums. The oil's natural antiseptic properties also help to prevent further infection and promote healing. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting healthy gums and preventing bleeding. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil can promote gum health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A terpene that helps reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums, thereby reducing bleeding.
Geraniol: A terpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums, which in turn reduces bleeding.
Limonene: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, and promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary source of essential oils in Cymbopogon citratus due to their high concentration of citral, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are another key source of essential oils from Cymbopogon citratus, as they contain citral and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent bleeding gums.

Stems: Stems are used to create essential oils that are rich in citral and other volatile compounds, which can help soothe and protect bleeding gums due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 fresh stalks of cymbopogon citratus, cutting them at an angle to prevent water from accumulating inside.
Steam distill the harvested stalks in a 2:1 water-to-plant ratio for 3-4 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled water and separate it from the essential oils using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil using a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing bleeding. Its calming effect can also help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are known contributors to gum recession and bleeding. By applying the oil topically or incorporating it into oral care routines, individuals can potentially alleviate bleeding gums and maintain a healthier smile. Regular use may also boost overall gum health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in the gums, thereby reducing bleeding.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the gums, reducing inflammation and bleeding.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing in the gums, thereby reducing bleeding and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal gum inflammation.
Leaves: Utilized for their antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can aid in reducing gum bleeding and promoting oral health.
Stems: Employed for their astringent properties, which can help contract and reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and color.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a still at 212 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water by decanting it into a separate container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining plant particles.
Bottle the final product in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place.
